mission.title: grpc: Support pre-built binaries for Ruby 3.1
mission.description: Support pre-built binaries for Ruby 3.1. Requested by 22+ users.

steps.title: Check current grpc setup
steps.description:
Verify your grpc version:
gRPC library — check your language-specific package (e.g., pip show grpcio, npm ls @grpc/grpc-js)
This feature requires grpc as a dependency.

steps.title: Review grpc configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant grpc configuration:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
It seems grpc does not support pre-built binaries for Ruby 3.1. This causes the gem-folder to balloon to almost 1gb.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support pre-built binaries for Ruby 3.1
steps.description:
If you want to save some space (in our case it's like 800MB) you can run the following after installing the gem:

```shell
grpc_path="$(bundle show --paths grpc)/src/ruby/ext/grpc"
make -C $grpc_path clean
rm -rf "$grpc_path"/{libs,objs}
```
```yaml
grpc_path="$(bundle show --paths grpc)/src/ruby/ext/grpc"
make -C $grpc_path clean
rm -rf "$grpc_path"/{libs,objs}
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Support pre-built binaries for Ruby 3.1" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
If you want to save some space (in our case it's like 800MB) you can run the following after installing the gem:

```shell
grpc_path="$(bundle show --paths grpc)/src/ruby/ext/grpc"
make -C $grpc_path clean
rm -rf "$grpc_path"/{libs,objs}
```

prerequisites.description: gRPC library — check your language-specific package (e.g., pip show grpcio, npm ls @grpc/grpc-js)
